input.text: Q3 planning meeting, Oct 5. Attendees: Sarah (PM), Raj (Engineering), Maya (Design). Sarah opened with the Q3 OKRs — three key results: ship the dashboard v2, reduce p95 latency to under 200ms, and launch the mobile beta. Raj said dashboard v2 is on track for end-of-October but the latency work depends on a database migration that hasn't been scheduled. Maya raised that the mobile beta UX hasn't been signed off by the design review board yet. Sarah took an action to schedule the database migration with the infra team this week. Maya will book the design review for next Tuesday. Raj asked whether the mobile beta should slip to Q4 if the design review reveals issues — left unresolved.

expected_output.summary: Q3 planning settled on three OKRs: dashboard v2 (on track for end of October), p95 latency under 200ms (blocked on an unscheduled database migration), and a mobile beta launch (blocked on design-review signoff). Two follow-up actions were assigned, plus an open question about the mobile beta's timing if design review surfaces issues.
key_points: Three Q3 OKRs agreed: dashboard v2, p95 latency under 200ms, mobile beta launch
Dashboard v2 is on track for end of October
Latency goal is blocked on an unscheduled database migration
Mobile beta is blocked on design-review signoff
action_items: Sarah to schedule the database migration with the infra team this week
Maya to book the design review for next Tuesday
open_questions: Should the mobile beta slip to Q4 if the design review reveals issues?

input.text:
From: legal@example.com
To: cto@example.com
Subject: Vendor agreement review

Reviewed the draft. Three issues: (1) Section 4.2 obligates us to a 99.99% uptime SLA without defining downtime — needs clarification. (2) Section 7 grants the vendor a perpetual license to our derivative works; we should narrow this to internal use only. (3) Section 11.3 caps their liability at $5K which is far below the contract value of $250K — needs negotiation. Otherwise the terms look standard. Happy to draft revisions if you confirm direction.

expected_output.summary: Legal reviewed the vendor agreement and flagged three contractual issues blocking signoff.
key_points: Three blocking issues: undefined downtime in the SLA, overly-broad derivative-works license, and liability cap far below contract value
action_items: CTO to confirm direction on the three issues so legal can draft revisions: (1) clarify SLA downtime definition, (2) narrow derivative-works license to internal use, (3) renegotiate liability cap from $5K toward $250K contract value
open_questions: What direction does the CTO want legal to take on each of the three flagged issues?

input.text:
Customer support ticket history for account #4421:
Oct 1: Customer reported login failure after password reset. Resolved by clearing browser cache.
Oct 8: Customer reported same login failure. Engineering investigated, found a session-cookie bug affecting Safari users. Hotfix deployed Oct 10.
Oct 11: Customer reported the issue is back. Investigation showed the hotfix didn't ship to the EU region. Re-deployed Oct 12.
Oct 14: Customer expressed frustration, asking for compensation. Escalated to customer success.

expected_output.summary: Account #4421 experienced repeated login failures across two weeks due to a Safari session-cookie bug that wasn't fully resolved on the first hotfix. The customer is now frustrated and has been escalated to customer success for relationship recovery.
key_points: Customer hit the same login bug 4 times across 2 weeks
Initial hotfix missed the EU region — second deployment was required
Customer is now frustrated and asking for compensation
action_items: Customer success to engage with the customer on compensation and relationship recovery
open_questions: Why did the initial hotfix not ship to the EU region — is this a deployment-process gap?
